title: sambucus sieboldiana f. latifolia
intro: Sambucus sieboldiana f. latifolia, also known as the Japanese elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The health benefits of Sambucus sieboldiana f. latifolia include its potential to reduce fever, relieve cold and flu symptoms, and soothe sore throats. The plant's berries and flowers are often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
